context.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations.
Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Confirm that a single legal parallel word produces a correct serial stream and a single valid_out pulse under conservative timing.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known non-control inputs: ['d']
Drive a nominal 4‑bit pattern (e.g., 0b1010) on d while reset is de‑asserted.
timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations.
Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Advance clock cycle‑by‑cycle and sample outputs after each rising edge; do not assume a fixed number of cycles before valid_out appears.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Exercise extreme and mixed bit‑patterns to verify width‑sensitive behavior without relying on exact latency.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['d']
Apply boundary patterns (0x0, 0xF, 0x1, 0x8) on d sequentially.
timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations.
Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe outputs over a conservative window of 6+ clock cycles after each pattern.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Observe repeated or back-to-back legal operations under conservative timing assumptions.
stimulus_intent: Apply two legal operations with minimal idle spacing that remains safe for the current contract strength.
timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations.
Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Validate the implicit valid‑ready protocol implied by valid_out without assuming a ready signal.
stimulus_intent: Apply first word 0b1100 on d, then after two clock cycles apply second word 0b0011 while the first word is still in progress.
timing_assumptions: Sample valid_out and dout each rising edge; do not assume a fixed latency for the second word's valid_out.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Observe module behavior under illegal/undefined input values while respecting contract ambiguity.
stimulus_intent: Drive d with a value containing X/Z bits (e.g., 4'b1x0z) and monitor outputs.
timing_assumptions: Observe for at least five clock cycles after stimulus; no fixed latency assumed.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Stress the design with multiple consecutive words to ensure state does not corrupt over time.
stimulus_intent: Apply a series of alternating patterns (0b1010, 0b0101) on d each cycle without gaps.
timing_assumptions: Sample each clock edge; do not assume a fixed latency between words.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

unresolved_items: Detected valid_ready-like signal(s) ['valid_out'] without matching role(s) ['ready'] in group 'out'.

Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: The most significant bit of the parallel input is assigned to the serial output (dout). On each clock cycle, if the counter (cnt) is 3, indicating the last bit of the parallel input, the module updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), resets the counter (cnt) to 0, and sets the valid signal (valid) to 1.
valid_ready ambiguity for valid_out without a matching ready signal.
Exact latency of valid_out assertion relative to input d is unspecified.

fill_warnings: Experimental TODO fill metadata is present, but the default mainline pipeline remains render -> run -> triage.

generated_files.description: Marks the rendered cocotb test directory as a Python package.
Conservative DUT interface helpers derived from the contract artifact.
Thin environment wrapper that composes interface, timing-safe waits, and coverage helpers.
Structured oracle helper functions rendered from the oracle artifact.
Stable runtime helpers exposed to LLM-filled stimulus and oracle TODO blocks.
Coverage scaffold rendered from plan coverage tags for later execution stages.
Rendered basic cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Rendered protocol cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Rendered edge cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Phase 4 executable Makefile shell; Phase 5 injects run-time variables and executes it.

unresolved_items: Detected valid_ready-like signal(s) ['valid_out'] without matching role(s) ['ready'] in group 'out'.

Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: The most significant bit of the parallel input is assigned to the serial output (dout). On each clock cycle, if the counter (cnt) is 3, indicating the last bit of the parallel input, the module updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), resets the counter (cnt) to 0, and sets the valid signal (valid) to 1.

unresolved_items: Detected valid_ready-like signal(s) ['valid_out'] without matching role(s) ['ready'] in group 'out'.

Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: The most significant bit of the parallel input is assigned to the serial output (dout). On each clock cycle, if the counter (cnt) is 3, indicating the last bit of the parallel input, the module updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), resets the counter (cnt) to 0, and sets the valid signal (valid) to 1.
valid_ready ambiguity for valid_out without a matching ready signal.
Exact latency of valid_out assertion relative to input d is unspecified.
Contract or plan confidence is limited; oracle generation is intentionally conservative and may omit value-level functional checks.
Hybrid LLM oracle augmentation produced no accepted checks beyond the baseline oracle.

render_warnings: Detected valid_ready-like signal(s) ['valid_out'] without matching role(s) ['ready'] in group 'out'.

Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: The most significant bit of the parallel input is assigned to the serial output (dout). On each clock cycle, if the counter (cnt) is 3, indicating the last bit of the parallel input, the module updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), resets the counter (cnt) to 0, and sets the valid signal (valid) to 1.
valid_ready ambiguity for valid_out without a matching ready signal.
Exact latency of valid_out assertion relative to input d is unspecified.
Contract or plan confidence is limited; oracle generation is intentionally conservative and may omit value-level functional checks.
Hybrid LLM oracle augmentation produced no accepted checks beyond the baseline oracle.
Oracle confidence is limited; rendered checks preserve unresolved items and avoid stronger value-level assertions.
Some rendered cases were downgraded to deferred execution because deterministic stimulus was not available: negative_001, regression_001
Rendered Makefile is an executable Phase 4 shell; Phase 5 injects execution variables and selects the backend.
Task description was provided to the render stage but not used to invent new testbench semantics.
Spec text was provided to the render stage but only upstream artifacts were trusted for code generation.
Deterministic render remains the default mainline; post-render TODO fill is experimental and not required by the benchmark path.
